test_output_summary: tests/test_phase2_dependencies.py: 41/41 pass. Full suite (uv run pytest): 504 passed in 4.04s, 55 warnings (pre-existing TAVILY_API_KEY UserWarnings, no regressions). ruff check on the new file: clean.
implementation_notes: Added Phase 2 runtime dependencies to [project].dependencies in pyproject.toml: langchain-tavily>=0.2 (DDR-006 web-search provider), asteval>=0.9.33 (DDR-007 safe expression evaluator with the floor recorded in DDR-007 §Consequences), and nats-core>=0.2 (Pydantic payload imports for CommandPayload / ResultPayload / BuildQueuedPayload — payloads only, no transport, per phase2-dispatch-foundations-scope §Scope Invariants ¶5). pyyaml was already pinned at >=6.0 from Phase 1, so AC-001's 'if not already present' clause was already satisfied. Phase 1 deps (deepagents, langchain-core, langgraph, langchain-openai, pydantic, pydantic-settings, structlog, python-dotenv, click, pyyaml) and the requires-python = '>=3.12,<3.13' pin are byte-for-byte unchanged; the [project.optional-dependencies].providers and [dependency-groups].dev sections are unchanged. ADR-ARCH-010 records that nats-core is 'installed from sibling repo (pip-installed wheel)'. The PyPI build of nats-core requires Python>=3.13 which collides with the 3.12 pin, so a [tool.uv.sources] table was added with nats-core = { path = '../nats-core', editable = true } pointing at the canonical sibling layout (jarvis/ ↔ nats-core/ as siblings under appmilla_github/). For the autobuild git worktree, where '../nats-core' would otherwise miss, a non-tracked symlink was placed at .guardkit/worktrees/nats-core so uv can resolve the path relative to the worktree's pyproject.toml without rewriting any committed file. uv lock regenerated cleanly (88 packages resolved; nats-py entered as a transitive of nats-core only — it is NOT in our pyproject.toml, satisfying the scope invariant which targets direct declarations). uv sync completed clean and a second run reports zero +/- lines (verified by TestAC003UvLockAndSync.test_uv_sync_completes_clean). Tests follow .claude/rules/testing.md class-per-AC structure with AAA naming (test_<thing>_<scenario>_<expected>).
concerns: nats-py is transitively installed via nats-core's own pyproject.toml (nats-core depends on nats-py>=2.0). The Phase 2 scope invariant explicitly targets direct declarations in *our* pyproject.toml ('does not appear in pyproject.toml dependencies until FEAT-JARVIS-004' — phase2-dispatch-foundations-scope), and AC-002 specifies 'nats-py is NOT added' (i.e. by us). That invariant is satisfied. The discipline of 'no NATS client imports in Phase 2' is a code-level rule that subsequent tasks (e.g. import-graph guards) will enforce against src/jarvis/ — out of scope for this dependency-management task.

[tool.uv.sources].nats-core uses path = '../nats-core' which works for the canonical sibling layout but not from a git worktree directly. The autobuild workflow handles this with a .guardkit/worktrees/nats-core symlink. A more robust long-term fix (uv workspace, or a packaging-time wheel) is left to a future task — this is acceptable for Phase 2 since ADR-ARCH-010 itself describes nats-core as 'installed from sibling repo'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `pyproject.toml` adds `langchain-tavily` (or the ADR-DDR-006-pinned equivalent), `asteval`, `nats-core` (for Pydantic payload imports), `pyyaml` if not already present.
completion_promises.evidence: Added langchain-tavily>=0.2 (DDR-006 chooses Tavily via langchain-tavily as the v1 web-search provider), asteval>=0.9.33 (DDR-007 §Consequences pins the floor at 0.9.33), and nats-core>=0.2 (DM-tool-types references nats_core.AgentManifest / ToolCapability / events) into [project].dependencies of pyproject.toml. pyyaml>=6.0 was already pinned in Phase 1 and is left in place. nats-core resolves to the sibling repo via [tool.uv.sources] per ADR-ARCH-010 ('installed from sibling repo (pip-installed wheel)'). TestAC001Phase2DependenciesAdded covers each package: parametrised test_dep_listed_in_runtime, test_langchain_tavily_pin_lower_bound, test_asteval_pin_satisfies_ddr_007 (asserts >=0.9.33), test_nats_core_path_source_for_sibling_install (asserts the [tool.uv.sources] table), test_pyyaml_present, and test_phase2_deps_importable (verifies they actually import after `uv sync`).

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `nats-py` (the NATS client library) is NOT added — Phase 2 scope invariant.
completion_promises.evidence: nats-py is absent from [project].dependencies, [project.optional-dependencies] (providers + any group), [dependency-groups].dev, and [tool.uv.sources] in pyproject.toml. TestAC002NatsPyNotDeclared has four tests covering each of those surfaces: test_nats_py_not_in_runtime_dependencies, test_nats_py_not_in_optional_dependencies, test_nats_py_not_in_dev_group, and test_uv_sources_does_not_pin_nats_py. The transitive presence of nats-py in the resolved venv (because nats-core itself depends on nats-py>=2.0) does not violate the scope invariant, which targets direct declarations in our pyproject.toml — phase2-dispatch-foundations-scope §Scope Invariants ¶5: 'nats-py does not appear in pyproject.toml dependencies until FEAT-JARVIS-004'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: [ ] `uv lock` is regenerated; `uv sync` completes clean.
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `uv lock` from the worktree: resolved 88 packages, added asteval, langchain-tavily, nats-core, plus their transitives (aiohttp, aiosignal, attrs, frozenlist, multidict, propcache, yarl, nats-py from nats-core's own pin). Ran `uv sync` immediately after: completed cleanly, second invocation reports zero `+`/`-` lines (verifying no drift). TestAC003UvLockAndSync covers this: test_uv_lock_exists checks the file is present, test_uv_lock_contains_phase2_packages greps the lockfile for `name = "<pkg>"` entries for langchain-tavily / asteval / nats-core, and test_uv_sync_completes_clean runs `uv sync` twice via subprocess and asserts the second run is a no-op (no '+' or '-' lines).

Every Phase 1 pin in [project].dependencies is byte-for-byte unchanged from the FEAT-JARVIS-001 / TASK-J001-001 baseline: deepagents>=0.5.3,<0.6 (ADR-ARCH-010), langchain-core>=0.3, langgraph>=0.3, langchain-openai>=0.2, pydantic>=2, pydantic-settings>=2, structlog>=24.1, python-dotenv>=1.0, click>=8.1, pyyaml>=6.0. The requires-python = '>=3.12,<3.13' pin is unchanged. [project.optional-dependencies].providers and [dependency-groups].dev are unchanged. Phase 1 modules (deepagents, langchain_core, langgraph, langchain_openai, pydantic, pydantic_settings, structlog, dotenv, click, yaml) all still import after the new pins, and `import jarvis` still produces version 0.1.0 in a subprocess. TestAC004Phase1DependenciesUntouched parametrises every Phase 1 pin via test_phase1_runtime_pin_unchanged, plus test_python_pin_unchanged, test_phase1_providers_untouched, test_phase1_dev_group_untouched, and test_project_metadata_untouched. TestPhase1ImportSurfaceStillWorks parametrises the 10 Phase 1 modules and verifies the jarvis package itself still imports cleanly.
